headline: Transcript: Luck makes careers. Here’s how to get more of it
summary: Isabel Berwick talks to Tomas Chamorro-Premuzic and Emma Jacobs
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Working It podcast episode: ‘Luck makes careers. Here’s how to get more of it’

Tomas Chamorro-PremuzicThere is no absolute objective, correct interpretation to how we got to where we are. But I think you can interpret your past accomplishments in a more ego-enhancing way or in a more punitive way. So I think it’s interesting to see how people make these interpretations irrespective of what the reality is, because that defines you.

Isabel BerwickHello and welcome to Working It from the Financial Times. I’m Isabel Berwick.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Successful people rarely admit how lucky they are. But luck plays a huge role in our professional success, perhaps a bigger role than talent or drive. So what does luck consist of? Is there anything we can do to make ourselves luckier? Or should we be looking to take luck out of the equation and focus on the things we can control? To find out, I’m speaking to Tomas Chamorro-Premuzic. Tomas, welcome to Working It.
